name: evocas9_filter_pipeline
description: Generate Cas9-bearing CRISPR loci with Evo1 (Nguyen et al. 2024, the 8k-context CRISPR-fine-tuned checkpoint `evo-1-8k-crispr` from Merchant et al. 2024) and filter them with rejection-sampling against three cheap->expensive registered constraints: (1) `mmseqs-gene-similarity` runs orfipy to extract ORFs and MMseqs2 (Steinegger & Soding 2017) to score percent identity against a Cas9 training-set protein database; we accept hits in 30-89% identity so the design is a recognizable Cas9 but not a verbatim copy of a training-set sequence; (2) `protein-domain` runs Prodigal + PyHMMER hmmscan against the Cas9 family profile (`cas9.hmm`, e.g. the TIGRFAM TIGR04330 model) and accepts only sequences with at least one significant Cas9 hit at E-value <=1e-3; (3) a second `protein-domain` scan against a multi-domain HMM library (`cas9_domains.hmm` with profiles RuvC_1, RuvC_2, RuvC_3, HNH) requires *all* canonical catalytic domains via `match_all_keywords: true` at E-value <=1e-10. Three additional filters that complete the full Cas9 pipeline (MinCED CRISPR-array detection, CRISPRtracrRNA + IntaRNA prediction, and an AF3-pLDDT + gyration-radius + longest-alpha-helix structure cluster) are not included here because they either rely on tools that are not yet exposed as registered constraints or require a DNA->protein translation step that the JSON parser does not chain inline; for the full pipeline see `examples/scripts/evocas9_rejection_sampling.py`. Set `mmseqs_db`, `hmm_db` paths to your local files before running.
